one_liner: Orchid Care reduces 30-day readmission for congestive heart failure patients via a remote-monitoring program.
use_of_funds_summary: Fund a 240-patient randomized trial across two partner health systems and a clinical operations director.
transcript_text: Orchid Care delivers a remote-monitoring care program for congestive heart failure patients in the 90 days after discharge. Our prior single-arm pilot enrolled 84 patients across two community hospitals from January through August. The 30-day readmission rate in the intervention arm was 11.9 percent compared with the historical baseline of 21.4 percent at the same hospitals over the prior 24 months. The pilot was registered as a quality improvement study under the partner health system institutional review boards. Average patient adherence to daily weight and symptom logging was 78 percent over the 90-day window. The 350,000 dollar check funds a 240-patient randomized controlled trial across the same two partner health systems and a clinical operations director for 12 months. We have signed letters of intent from both partners and have completed the protocol pre-registration. Our gross margin per enrolled patient at scale is 62 percent based on the pilot cost of 410 dollars per patient against a contracted reimbursement of 1,080 dollars. Our team includes a board-certified cardiologist, a clinical trial operations lead, and an engineering team that previously shipped the partner hospital integration.
